project_type: Python Telegram bot for OMR (Optical Mark Recognition) test grading
build_system: pip with requirements.txt, no Makefile or build scripts beyond deploy.sh
test_framework: No formal test framework - manual testing via Telegram bot interaction
test_command: python3 -c "from sheet_generator import generate_answer_sheet; from omr_scanner import scan_answer_sheet; img = generate_answer_sheet(10, 4); img.save('/tmp/test_sheet.png'); print('Import and generation OK')"
verification_instructions: 1. Install requirements. 2. Verify imports work. 3. Generate a test sheet and confirm it has ArUco markers. 4. Run a synthetic scan test: generate a sheet, fill in some bubbles programmatically, save it, scan it with scan_answer_sheet() and verify correct results.
setup_instructions: pip install -r requirements.txt (in the test_grader_bot directory)
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - can install packages. OpenCV 4.13.0 with cv2.aruco module available (ArucoDetector, DICT_4X4_50, generateImageMarker). qrcode library needs to be installed.
contribution_requirements: bot.py must NOT be modified. Function signatures of generate_answer_sheet() and scan_answer_sheet() must remain the same. All UI text in Uzbek.
key_patterns: - sheet_generator.py exports constants (SHEET_WIDTH, SHEET_HEIGHT, MARKER_SIZE, etc.) that omr_scanner.py imports for coordinate calculations. - scan_answer_sheet() returns dict with keys: answers, score, total, details, student_number, uncertain_questions, error. - generate_answer_sheet() returns PIL Image. - 3-method consensus voting (A=adaptive threshold relative, B=Otsu circular, C=morphological) for bubble detection. - Two-column layout when questions > 20. - Student number section with tens (0-4) and units (0-9) rows.
directory_structure: test_grader_bot/ - main code directory with bot.py (telegram bot logic), omr_scanner.py (image scanning), sheet_generator.py (PDF/image generation), config.py (env config), excel_export.py (results export), requirements.txt
